content.text: I need a way to load a raster image into an object, whether I have its file path or just a stream of raw data.
If the input is a stream providing large amounts of data, could there be any risk of excessive memory usage?

Yes, when reading from a file-like object like this, all `imgdata` must first be loaded into RAM. For very large datasets, it might be better to implement chunked or streaming readers outside of this function.
That makes sense; I appreciate the warning.
